name: tabs
description: WAI-ARIA APG Tabs: a role=tablist strip of role=tab triggers + role=tabpanel regions. Controlled/uncontrolled headless primitive -- the active tab is server state passed via the plain `activeTab` @param; the caller binds it to their own @Wire field. No @Wire field of its own, no Java component. ENH: collection-nav.enhancer.ts in roving-tabindex mode handles ArrowLeft/Right/Up/Down, Home/End, Enter/Space (manual mode), automatic activation. Supports sm/md/lg sizes (toolbar-aligned), line/card/pill types, eager (all panels in DOM) or lazy HTMX panel swap, closable tabs, addable tab button, manual/automatic activation modes. data-slots: tabs / tabs-list / tabs-trigger / tabs-close / tabs-add / tabs-content. Pure HTML + --lv-* tokens, zero inline script, zero on*= handlers, CSP-clean.
docs:
Copies the headless controlled/uncontrolled tabs primitive to lievit/tabs.jte. Call @template.lievit.tabs(tabsId=..., tabIds=..., tabLabels=..., activeTab=..., tabsLabel=..., selectAction="activate"). The tabsId param is REQUIRED and must be unique on the page -- derive it from your wire component id (e.g. _component.id()) so the ARIA anchors are deterministic. Pass tabIcons / tabClosable / tabDisabled as parallel lists (index-aligned with tabIds; may be shorter -- missing entries default to no icon / false / false). For controlled use: bind activeTab to your @Wire field and set selectAction to your activate action. For lazy HTMX panels: set lazyLoad=true and provide a GET endpoint at /tabs/{tabsId}/panel/{tabId} returning the panel fragment. The panel body regions in this copied-in template are OWNED markup: replace the placeholder

per tab with your real server-rendered content. collection-nav.enhancer.ts must be installed on the runtime (installCollectionNav(runtime)) for arrow-key roving and automatic/manual activation.
